Tnc Of Mtn To Compel Further Answers To Form And Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Nov-23-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Susan K. Riggio for defendant and moving party: St. Luke's Hospital
Christine M. Meade for defendant and opposing party: Lisa Naguchi

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel further answers to form and special interrogatories as to defendant Lisa Noguchi: Granted in part and denied in part.
The objections to the form interrogatories are overruled. Defendant is ordered to respond by December 14, 2005.
Regarding the special interrogatories:
1,2: denied. The interrogatories are compound
3, 4, 5: denied, as defendant has served supplemental responses
6,7: denied. The respective interrogatories are compound and not full and complete in and of themselves.
8: denied
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Amswers To Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Nov-23-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Susan K. Riggio for defendant and moving party: St. Luke's Hospital
John Farrell for plaintiff and opposing party: Mireya Poblano, CArmen Poblano, Jose Poblano, Carole Maya and Veronica Maya

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel further answers to form interrogatories to defendant St. Luke's Hospital: GRANTED.
The objections are overruled. As the response reserves the right to amend the response is ordered stricken. The response must be full and complete at the time it is made. If there is a need to amend, it must be made in compliance with CCP Section 2030.310. Defendant is ordered to respond by December 14, 2005. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Off Calendar Oct-26-2005 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS MIREYA POBLANO, CARMEN POBLANO, JOSE POBLANO, CAROLE MAYA AND VERONICA MAYA'S Motion To Compel Further Answers To Special Interrogatories & Further Responses To Demand For Production.
RULING: RENOTICED FOR THE DISCOVERY DEPARTMENT. (302/REQ). Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Reporter: Beldon Yee, CSR #2647

(A M E N D E D) Ntc Of Mo To Compel Further Ans To Special Interrogs & Further Responses To Demand For Production
Off Calendar Oct-26-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John J. Farrell for plaintiff and moving party: Mireya Poblano
Maureen Loftis for defendant and opposing party: Maureen H. Loftis

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to interrogatories and request for production of documents:
Interrogatories
17 - 18:
Denied. The interrogatories request information that is not likely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence
19 - 26:
Denied. While the Code of Civil Procedure permits contention interrogatories, as drafted the interrogatories are vague and ambiguous and may contravene the statutory scheme set forth in CCP Section 2034 et seq.

Request for production
3:
Granted. If defendant has previously produced all responsive documents, that representation shall be verified.
4:
Denied. The request seeks information that is not likely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence
5:
Denied as moot. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
